home.subtitle: Self-hosted Minecraft skin site and authlib-injector compatible authentication server.
home.heroCopy: AsterYggdrasil provides secure, stable, and fast Yggdrasil authentication with easy skin management for a complete Minecraft experience.
home.featureSecureDescription: Yggdrasil-compatible authentication keeps account access controlled.
home.featureProfilesDescription: Keep Minecraft names, UUIDs, and account ownership under your own service.
home.featureTexturesDescription: Upload, manage, and share Minecraft skins and capes after signing in.
home.featureAuthDescription: Use site accounts for authlib-injector compatible launcher login.
home.featureStableDescription: Low-latency authentication paths are designed for high availability.
home.featureControlDescription: Runtime settings, keys, and audit records stay behind the admin workbench.
home.audienceDescription: Whether you run a server, build integrations, or just want a clean skin workflow, AsterYggdrasil keeps the core tools close at hand.
home.audienceServerDescription: Provide reliable Yggdrasil authentication for Minecraft server operators.
home.audienceDeveloperDescription: Clear API endpoints and integration values make deployment easier.
home.audienceSkinDescription: Rich skin and cape management helps players express their style.
home.audienceCommunityDescription: Support shared communities with controlled account and profile workflows.

tos.heading: Terms of Service
tos.lead: By using this site account, Minecraft profiles, texture wardrobe, public texture library, or Yggdrasil authentication endpoints, you agree to these Terms of Service and any supplemental rules published by the site administrators.

body.0: This site provides a self-hosted Minecraft skin site, account workbench, player profile management, texture upload and preview, public texture library, and authlib-injector compatible Yggdrasil authentication. The exact available features depend on the configuration enabled by this site's administrators.
body.1: This site is not an official Mojang, Microsoft, or Minecraft service and does not replace the official account system. Minecraft names, trademarks, game clients, official skin services, and related assets remain owned by their respective rights holders.

body.0: You should use an email address, username, password, passkey, external login identity, or invitation link that you control. You are responsible for player profiles, launcher sessions, texture uploads, reports, and administration actions made through your account.
body.1: Do not impersonate others, steal accounts, share sensitive credentials, sell accounts, register in bulk, abuse invitations, bypass CAPTCHA or access controls, or use automated requests in a way that harms authentication, texture delivery, mail, or background task stability.
body.2: Administrators may require password changes or email verification, revoke sessions, disable accounts, revoke invitations, adjust user roles, or restrict operator scopes according to the site's security policy.

body.0: You may create, rename, or delete Minecraft player profiles under your account. Profile names, UUIDs, bound textures, and audit records are used for launcher login, server session verification, and public profile presentation.
body.1: Profile names must not impersonate others, create confusion, violate community rules, or infringe third-party rights. Administrators may rename, freeze, or delete profiles for security, compliance, abuse handling, or dispute resolution; renames and deletion may invalidate related Yggdrasil tokens.

body.0: Skins, capes, avatars, or other image content you upload should be content you are allowed to use, and must satisfy this site's validation rules for file size, MIME type, PNG format, dimensions, model type, name, and tags.
body.1: Do not upload content that is illegal, infringing, malicious, hateful, harassing, sexual, threatening, doxxing, misleadingly impersonating, or clearly harmful to the community experience.
body.2: Textures may be private or public depending on your settings, bound profiles, public library status, and administrator actions. To let Minecraft clients, launchers, and servers read them correctly, bound or public texture URLs may be publicly accessible, previewed, cached, or copied.

library.title: 5. Public Texture Library, Review, and Reports

body.0: When you submit a texture to the public texture library, you allow this site to display that texture, its name, type, model, tags, hash, dimensions, uploader information, and previews, and to let other users copy it to their own wardrobe when the feature permits.
body.1: Administrators may review, approve, reject, unpublish, reclassify, or adjust public texture library entries and tags according to site rules. Review notes, report reasons, handling results, and related actions may be stored in audit records.
body.2: You may report violating public library content, but you must not report maliciously, harass repeatedly, fabricate evidence, or use the reporting workflow to attack other users. Administrators may accept or reject reports and retain handling records as needed.

body.0: This site's Yggdrasil endpoints, authlib-injector add-server URI, texture signatures, sessionserver lookups, join/hasJoined checks, and launcher upload endpoints are provided only for compatible Minecraft launchers, clients, servers, and related tools.
body.1: Do not disclose access tokens, client tokens, browser sessions, passkeys, or other authentication material to others. Do not forge requests, replay tokens, bypass rate limits, scan private endpoints, or interfere with other players' login flow.
body.2: Protocol compatibility may change with Minecraft, authlib-injector, or this site. Administrators may limit, pause, refresh, or invalidate sessions for security, maintenance, key rotation, profile changes, or abuse handling.

body.0: Do not attempt unauthorized access, privilege escalation, injection, brute force, denial of service, CSRF/CORS/rate-limit bypass, malformed file abuse, cache poisoning, token theft, non-public data scraping, or audit trail tampering.
body.1: If you discover a vulnerability, contact an administrator promptly without expanding impact, accessing unrelated data, or publishing exploit details. Administrators may temporarily restrict accounts, IPs, endpoints, or related features while investigating risk.

body.0: Administrators and authorized operators may manage users, invitations, external login providers, runtime configuration, CAPTCHA, mail, signing keys, the texture library, reports, background tasks, system information, and audit records within their granted permissions.
body.1: The service may be temporarily unavailable during upgrades, incidents, migrations, backups, third-party outages, configuration errors, or security events. Administrators should reduce impact where practical, but uninterrupted, error-free, or universal client compatibility is not guaranteed.
body.2: To the extent permitted by law, the site is provided as is. Losses caused by your violation of these terms, use of untrusted clients, credential disclosure, prohibited uploads, or reliance on unstable interfaces are your responsibility.

body.0: Administrators may update these terms as features, deployment environments, community rules, security requirements, or legal requirements change. Material changes should be announced through the site interface, notices, or community channels.
body.1: If you do not agree with updated terms, stop using the site and contact an administrator about your account, player profiles, textures, public library entries, or other related data.

body.0: Questions about accounts, texture rights, report handling, data requests, or security issues should be directed to this site's administrators with enough information to locate the issue, such as account name, profile UUID, texture ID, report ID, or time of occurrence.
body.1: These terms are site service rules and are not legal advice. If the administrators publish more specific community rules, server rules, or compliance notices, those rules should be read together with these terms.

privacy.heading: Privacy Policy
privacy.lead: This policy explains what data this site handles for account login, Minecraft player profiles, texture hosting, the public texture library, report review, and Yggdrasil protocol compatibility.

body.0: This site may store usernames, email addresses, email verification status, password hashes, roles, operator scopes, avatars, language preferences, account status, invitation records, and security settings.
body.1: This site may store Minecraft player profile names, UUIDs, profile ownership, skin and cape bindings, texture files, texture names, types, models, hashes, dimensions, MIME types, tags, visibility, public library status, review notes, report reasons, and report handling records.
body.2: The server may also record IP addresses, user agents, request times, request paths, login sessions, necessary Yggdrasil token verification data, administration actions, error logs, background task state, mail delivery records, and audit events.

body.0: Browser login generally relies on secure cookies, CSRF tokens, and necessary local cache to maintain sessions, reduce repeated requests, and show recent user state. The site may also store session expiry time, device names, and token hashes.
body.1: If you use passkeys or external login, this site stores the public credential identifiers, provider kind, external identity identifiers, link state, and last-use times needed to complete authentication. The site should not store raw biometric data or third-party account passwords.

body.0: Player profile and texture data is used so launchers, Minecraft clients, and servers can identify your character, read skins and capes, verify sessionserver responses, and display preview and management state in the workbench.
body.1: Texture previews, avatar rendering, image validation, and public library display may produce derived images, cache entries, or statistics. Deletion, rename, or unpublishing actions may not immediately disappear from every client cache, browser cache, or reverse proxy cache.

body.0: This data is used for account registration and login, invitation acceptance, password reset, email verification, passkey management, external login linking, launcher authentication, profile management, texture upload, texture delivery, public library review, report handling, permission control, notifications, security audit, and troubleshooting.
body.1: Administrators should not use data for purposes unrelated to site identity, texture, authentication, community governance, and service operations unless you have consented, it is necessary to protect users or the service, or the law requires it.

body.0: Player profile names, UUIDs, and bound skins or capes may be visible through Yggdrasil metadata, sessionserver responses, texture URLs, public read endpoints, launchers, Minecraft clients, and servers.
body.1: Approved public texture library entries may show texture names, types, models, tags, previews, uploader public identifiers, creation times, and update times, and may be copied by other users into their own wardrobe.
body.2: Do not upload texture content that you do not want other players, servers, launchers, browser caches, CDNs, reverse proxies, or search and archive tools to see.

body.0: This site records security and operational events such as login, logout, token refresh, password change, passkey operations, external login linking, profile changes, texture upload/bind/delete, public library submission/review/unpublishing, report submission/handling, configuration changes, mail delivery, and task retries.
body.1: Audit records may include the actor, target object, time, action type, result, short details, and presentation metadata. Administrators use these records to troubleshoot issues, handle abuse, trace permissioned actions, and satisfy necessary compliance requirements.

body.0: Passwords, Yggdrasil tokens, session secrets, client tokens, signing private keys, and other sensitive material should be stored only as hashes, encrypted values, or the minimum form required to run the service. Plain credentials should not be written to logs, audit records, or error messages.
body.1: If you suspect your account, tokens, email address, passkey, or external login identity have been compromised, change your password, revoke other sessions, delete suspicious passkeys or external links, and contact a site administrator.

body.0: This site does not actively sell your personal data. Databases, object storage, cache services, mail services, CAPTCHA components, reverse proxies, logging systems, backup systems, or hosting environments required to run the service may process necessary data.
body.1: If you use external login, the relevant provider may process your login request, identity identifier, callback address, and authorization state under its own policies. This site only controls the data it stores and processes.
body.2: When required for law, security incidents, abuse handling, rights complaints, community governance, or service migration, administrators may inspect, export, or provide relevant logs, account records, texture records, and report records.

body.0: Accounts, player profiles, textures, public library entries, reports, audit records, mail records, and logs are retained according to site configuration, backup cycles, administrator policy, and applicable rules. Some security logs, audit records, backups, and caches may remain for a period after account, profile, or texture deletion.
body.1: Because texture URLs, public library content, and Yggdrasil responses may be cached by clients, servers, browsers, or proxies, deletion or unpublishing does not guarantee that data already delivered to external environments becomes unavailable immediately.

body.0: You can manage account details, password, sessions, passkeys, external login links, player profiles, texture wardrobe, tags, and public library submissions in the workbench when the corresponding features are enabled by administrators.
body.1: You may contact an administrator to request access, correction, export, deletion, or restriction of data related to your account. Administrators will handle requests according to identity verification, site capabilities, security requirements, backup limits, and applicable rules.

body.0: For privacy, data deletion, security incidents, texture rights, or report handling questions, contact this site's administrators and provide an account, email address, profile UUID, texture ID, report ID, or relevant time range so the issue can be located.
body.1: Administrators may update this policy as features, deployment environments, data processing methods, or legal requirements change. Continued use of the site means you have been informed of the updated data handling notice.

errorPage.notFoundDescription: Looks like this path points outside the loaded world.
errorPage.genericDescription: The current view failed before it could render. You can return home or go back and try again.
errorPage.reasonMoved: The page may have moved or been deleted
errorPage.reasonPermission: You may not have permission to access this page
errorPage.helpDescription: If you think this is a mistake, include the error code when contacting the site administrator.

launcher.title: Launcher access
launcher.description: Add this authentication server in your launcher, then sign in with your site account.
launcher.userTitle: Add this site to your launcher
launcher.profileTitle: Use a profile in your launcher
launcher.userDescription: Add this authentication server in a launcher that supports authlib-injector, then sign in with your site account and choose your Minecraft profile.
launcher.externalLoginName: Choose external login in the launcher
launcher.externalLoginDescription: Use your site account username or email and password.
launcher.apiRootHint: If the launcher asks for an authentication server address, paste the displayed API Root.
launcher.selectProfile: Choose your Minecraft profile after signing in
launcher.selectProfileName: Choose the profile you want after signing in
launcher.selectProfileDescription: Profiles, skins, and capes are resolved from this site.
launcher.profileHint: Profiles and skins are managed here; the launcher only signs in and starts the game.
launcher.dragHint: Launchers that support drag-and-drop server setup will recognize this authlib-injector address.
launcher.serverOwnerDescription: Place authlib-injector.jar in the server directory, add the JVM argument before -jar, and keep online-mode=true.
launcher.secureProfileDescription: authlib-injector v1.2.0 and newer should keep secure profile checks enabled. Keep these server.properties values, then use the same launch command.
launcher.connectionDescription: Players only need this address when their launcher cannot import it by drag-and-drop.
launcher.domainsDescription: Skin and cape URLs are resolved automatically after launcher login.

init.title: Finish initial setup
init.headline: Finish Aster initial setup
init.description: No administrator account exists yet. Create the first admin and set the public address people use to reach this site.
init.cardDescription: This account becomes the first administrator. The public site URL is used to build launcher setup URLs and texture URLs.
init.publicSiteUrlHelp: Enter the site root that players, launchers, and Minecraft clients can reach. Use only scheme and host, with an optional port, and no path.
init.publicSiteUrlInvalid: The public site URL is not valid.
init.publicSiteUrlProtocol: The public site URL must use http or https.
init.publicSiteUrlOriginOnly: The public site URL must be the site root without a path, query, or hash.
init.publicSiteUrlHttpWarning: Use HTTPS for public deployments. HTTP is intended for localhost or private-network testing.
init.featureAdminDescription: Manage runtime settings, profiles, textures, and audit records after setup.
init.featureUrlDescription: The site, Yggdrasil API, and texture URLs are derived from one public site URL.
init.featurePlayerDescription: Texture domains include this site plus the official Minecraft and Mojang domains.

login.title: Login
login.welcomeTitle: Welcome back. Sign in to your Aster account.
login.welcomePrefix: Welcome back. Sign in to your
login.welcomeDescription: Manage Minecraft profiles, skins, capes, launcher sessions, and account permissions from your workspace.
login.headline: Manage Minecraft profiles and textures after signing in.
login.setupHeadline: Initialize this Yggdrasil server before players sign in.
login.description: Site accounts control profile ownership, texture uploads, external auth bindings, and admin access. Launcher protocol login still uses the Yggdrasil API.
login.setupDescription: No administrator account exists yet. Create the first admin account to finish setup.
login.cardDescription: Use your site account to enter the workbench. Profile-name login is handled by launcher protocol endpoints.
login.setupCardDescription: This account becomes the first administrator and can manage runtime config, profiles, textures, and audit records.
login.registerDescription: Fill in the fields below to create your Aster account.
login.registerHeadline: Join Aster and start your Minecraft identity workspace.
login.registerHeroDescription: Create an account to manage profiles, textures, permissions, and account security from one place.
login.externalOnlyTitle: Sign in with an external account
login.externalOnlyDescription: Local username and password login is disabled. Use an external identity provider enabled by the administrator.
login.externalOnlyUnavailable: No external login provider is available. Ask an administrator to configure the LinuxDo provider.
login.captchaUnavailable: Verification image failed to load. Refresh and try again.
login.usernamePlaceholder: 4-16 characters: letters, numbers, underscores, and hyphens
login.validationFailed: Check the highlighted fields and try again.
login.validationUsernameChars: Username may only contain letters, numbers, underscores, and hyphens.
login.validationAcceptTerms: Confirm that this account is used for Aster authentication management.
login.acceptTerms: I understand this account is used for Aster profile, texture, and authentication management.
login.registerActivationSent: Account created. Check your email to activate it.
login.activationSuccess: Email verified. You can sign in now.
login.contactVerificationInvalid: The verification link is invalid or has already been used.
login.contactVerificationExpired: The verification link has expired. Request a new one.
login.contactVerificationMissing: The verification link is missing its token.
login.passwordResetRequestDescription: Enter your account email and we will send a password reset message. To prevent account enumeration, the response does not reveal whether the account exists.
login.passwordResetRequested: If the email exists, a reset message will be sent to that address.
login.resetPasswordDescription: Set a new sign-in password. Other active sessions will be invalidated.
login.passwordResetSuccess: Password reset. Sign in with your new password.
login.forcePasswordChangeDescription: An administrator requires you to change your sign-in password before continuing to the workbench. A normal session will be issued after it succeeds.
login.validationPasswordSameAsCurrent: New password cannot match the current password.
login.resetPasswordInvalidDescription: This link does not exist, has already been used, or is not a password reset link.
login.resetPasswordExpiredDescription: This password reset link has expired. Request a new email.
login.passkeyUnsupported: Passkeys are not supported by this browser.
login.externalAuthRecoveryDescription: This external identity's email cannot sign in directly yet. Link it with an existing site account password, or verify the email to finish creating or linking the account.
login.externalAuthEmailSentHint: Open the link in the email to finish verification. After it succeeds, you will return to the login flow.
login.externalAuthEmailMissing: External auth email verification link is missing its token.
login.externalAuthEmailInvalid: External auth email verification link is invalid or has already been used.
login.externalAuthEmailExpired: External auth email verification link expired. Start the external sign-in again.
login.externalAuthFailed: External authentication failed. Start the sign-in again.
login.featureSecureDescription: Use site accounts with Yggdrasil-compatible launcher authentication.
login.featureProfileDescription: Manage Minecraft profile ownership, skins, and capes after signing in.
login.featureAuditDescription: Admin actions and sensitive changes stay traceable through audit logs.
login.registerFeatureSecureDescription: Use Yggdrasil-compatible authentication to protect your account.
login.registerFeatureProfileDescription: Manage Minecraft profiles and personal texture assets after signing in.
login.registerFeatureAuditDescription: Permissions and security-sensitive changes are handled inside the workbench.
login.registerConsentHint: Registration means your account joins this Aster authentication server.

invite.headline: Accept your invitation and create a site account.
invite.heroDescription: Use this invitation link to join the Aster authentication server. After creating an account, you can manage profiles, textures, and account security from the workbench.
invite.cardDescription: {{email}} was invited to join this server. The invitation expires at {{expiresAt}}.
invite.cardDescriptionEmail: {{email}} was invited to join this server.
